overview: This image features a margay (Leopardus wiedii), a small wild cat native to Central and South America. Margays are renowned for their incredible climbing abilities, often called "tree ocelots," and can rotate their hind feet 180 degrees to climb down trees headfirst. They are nocturnal, solitary animals that primarily inhabit dense primary evergreen and deciduous forests. The species is currently listed as Near Threatened by the IUCN due to habitat loss and fragmentation. Wikipedia +4 5 sites Margay - Wikipedia
